data.title: Is United Parcel Stock (UPS) a Buy Ahead of Q4 Earnings?
data.description:
Parcel carrier United Parcel (UPS) is set to report its Q4 earnings this week. Ahead of those results should investors get carried away or return its shares to sender?

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What Wall Street Expects

Wall Street expects UPS to announce quarterly earnings of $2.22 per share in its report this week, representing a decline of 19.3% year over year. Revenues are projected to reach $24.01 billion, declining 5.1% from the same quarter last year.

Will UPS beat these estimates? As can be seen below, it has a good track record of doing just that in recent quarters.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: This Is Why Brand Engagement Network Stock (BNAI) Rocketed Today
data.description:
Brand Engagement Network (BNAI) stock was on the rise again Monday after the artificial intelligence (AI) company caught the interest of retail traders. Increased trading activity has been ongoing for BNAI stock after the company announced the finalization of a strategic partnership with Valio Technologies last week.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This agreement will have Brand Engagement Network establish an exclusive AI licensing framework for government and commercial markets across Africa. This includes a memorandum of understanding with Nelson Mandela University for a "regulated, institution-approved pilot deployment of artificial intelligence designed to enhance student wellbeing."

Retail traders appear to have taken notice of this deal in early morning trading on Monday, resulting in heightened trading activity today. As of this writing, more than 3.9 million shares have changed hands. While this is still below the company's three-month daily average of 14.26 million units, investors will note that this movement came before the opening bell today. Additionally, the stock saw some 44 million shares change hands on Friday.

Brand Engagement Network Stock Movement Today

Brand Engagement Network stock was up 241.08% in pre-market trading on Monday, following a 90.3% rally on Friday. The shares have increased 610.34% year-to-date and 244.84% over the past 12 months. Investors will also note that the stock has rallied 171.95% over the past five days.
data.author: William White

data.title: Stock Bulls Rejoice as ServiceNow Selloff 'Looks Overdone,' Says Analyst
data.description:
ServiceNow (NOW) shares have fallen more than 40% over the past 12 months, sharply lagging the broader market, as the S&P 500 (SPX) is up roughly 14% over the same period. With expectations reset ahead of earnings and the company continuing to execute at a high level, I view the current environment as an attractive entry point and remain bullish on the stock.

data.title: XOM or CVX: Which Energy Stock Does Wall Street Favor Ahead of Q4 Earnings?
data.description:
Oil and gas giants Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Chevron (CVX) are scheduled to announce their results for the fourth quarter of 2025 on January 30. Lower commodity prices are expected to weigh on the Q4 results of energy companies. That said, several analysts remain optimistic about the solid execution and long-term growth prospects of Exxon Mobil and Chevron. data.description:

Here are today's top analyst-backed stock picks. Click on any ticker to research the company before deciding whether to add it to your portfolio.

Exagen (XGN) – Exagen develops blood-based diagnostic tests for autoimmune diseases such as lupus and rheumatoid arthritis. Today, Canaccord Genuity analyst Kyle Mikson maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a price target of $15 per share. In the last three months, all four Top Analysts covering the stock have rated it a Buy. Taken together, their 12-month price targets imply an upside of about 204.35%.

Vertical Aerospace (EVTL) – Vertical Aerospace designs and develops electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft for urban air mobility and short-haul air travel. Today, William Blair analyst Louie DiPalma maintained a Buy rating on the stock. In the last three months, all three Top Analysts covering the stock have rated it a Buy. Taken together, their 12-month price targets imply an upside of about 96.43%.

Par Technology (PAR) – PAR Technology provides cloud-based software and hardware solutions for restaurants and hospitality businesses. Today, BTIG analyst Andrew Harte maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a price target of $60 per share. In the last three months, all four Top Analysts covering the stock have rated it a Buy. Taken together, their 12-month price targets imply an upside of about 101.74%.

data.title: USA Rare Earth (USAR) Stock Rallies on $1.6 Billion CHIPS Program Support
data.description:
In a move with clear implications for chips, EVs, and AI, USA Rare Earth Inc. (USAR) said it has reached a non-binding agreement with the U.S. government for major funding support. The plan includes up to $1.6 billion through the Commerce Department CHIPS Program, according to the company. As a result, investors have reacted positively, driving the stock up nearly 20% (at the time of writing) in pre-market trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The package would include about $277 million in direct funding and a $1.3 billion senior loan. In return, the U.S. government would receive shares and warrants that could lead to an ownership stake of about 8% to 16% on a fully diluted basis. However, the company said the deal is still subject to review, final terms, and strict goals tied to project progress.

At the same time, USA Rare Earth announced a separate $1.5 billion private funding round. The company plans to issue about 69.8 million shares at $21.50 per share, with the deal set to close on January 28, 2026. The funds will be used for general company needs and to support long-term growth plans.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Why Is Battalion Oil Stock (BATL) Up Today?
data.description:
Battalion Oil (BATL) stock rocketed higher on Monday after the independent energy company announced a new gas treating agreement. This agreement is with a publicly traded large-cap midstream company. Battalion Oil sought a new partner for this, after operations at its partner Wink Amine Treater's acid gas injection facility ended on August 11, 2025.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

With the end of those operations, Battalion Oil has exercised its contractual rights to terminate the gas treating agreement with Wink Amine Treater. Battalion Oil didn't name the company in its new gas treating agreement, but it did note that the company's facility is able to handle all of its gas volumes from its Monument Draw Field.

Battalion Oil noted that the updated gas treating agreement is a major benefit to its operations. It listed additional flow assurance and operational reliability among these benefits, and stated that it has allowed the company to increase average oil production to approximately 1,200 net barrels of oil per day in January when compared to December.

Battalion Oil Stock Movement Today

Battalion Oil stock was up 319.53% in pre-market trading on Monday, following a 4.92% rally on Friday. However, the shares were still down 30.43% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy pre-market trading of BATL stock, as more than 107 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 77,000 units.
data.author: William White

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Injects Another $2B in CoreWeave to Speed Up Global AI Factory Buildout
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) has injected an additional $2 billion into AI cloud provider CoreWeave (CRWV) as part of an expanded partnership to speed up the construction of global AI factory infrastructure. The companies are collaborating to build AI factories, aiming for over 5 gigawatts of capacity by 2030.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As part of the deal, Nvidia purchased $2 billion of CoreWeave Class A common stock at $87.20 per share.

This investment deepens the already close relationship between the two, with CoreWeave serving as a specialized cloud provider for Nvidia's advanced GPUs (including future Rubin and Blackwell platforms).

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock based on 39 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations. The average NVDA stock price target of $263.44 indicates 40.37% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: CoreWeave Stock Jumps 9% on $2B Nvidia Investment as Vera Chip Debuts
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) is strengthening its grip on the AI world by sinking another $2 billion into CoreWeave (CRWV), one of its closest partners. This massive investment helps CoreWeave build out AI factories with enough power to rival five nuclear reactors. But the real surprise in this deal is the debut of Vera, a new Nvidia chip designed to challenge Intel (INTC) and AMD (AMD) for the "brain" of the data center.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia Attacks the CPU Market

For years, Nvidia has dominated the market for GPUs, the powerful chips that do the heavy lifting for AI. Now, with the launch of the Vera CPU, Nvidia is going after the host processors that have traditionally been sold by Intel and AMD. This is the first time Nvidia has offered a CPU on its own, meaning a company can now build a complete data center using only Nvidia-branded chips.

Jensen Huang described the Vera chip as "completely revolutionary" because it is built specifically to manage the fast-moving data that AI requires. By cutting out the need for chips from other companies, Nvidia can make sure its systems work faster and use less power. This move puts immediate pressure on traditional chip giants who are already struggling to keep up with the AI boom.

CoreWeave Builds the Power Grid

CoreWeave is using the new $2 billion from Nvidia to fuel an incredible construction project. The goal is to reach 5 gigawatts of computing capacity by 2030, enough electricity to power nearly 4 million homes. CoreWeave acts as a specialized neocloud, providing the massive clusters of chips that startups like OpenAI and xAI need to train their next-generation models.

This partnership is about more than just money; it is a deep engineering bond. CoreWeave will be the first to test and use Nvidia's latest tech, including the Vera Rubin superchip platform. While some investors worry about the circular nature of Nvidia investing in its own customers, the company argues that this is the only way to build the massive amount of infrastructure the world currently demands.

Investors Balance Risk and Reward

The news sent CoreWeave's stock climbing 11% as it helps ease worries about the company's heavy debt. CoreWeave has been spending billions of dollars on land and power faster than it brings in revenue, which has made some traders nervous. Having the world's most valuable company as a major shareholder provides a safety net that suggests CoreWeave's aggressive growth plan is working.

Meanwhile, Nvidia's own forecast for 2026 remains incredibly bullish. The company expects to see half a trillion dollars in revenue from data center chips alone by the end of next year. By expanding into CPUs and storage, Nvidia is making sure that even if the competition gets better at making GPUs, they will still have to pay Nvidia for the rest of the computer.

Investors can compare AI stocks side-by-side on the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Tool. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani
